dealership.market_signal: CarMax $2.4B, Carvana AI pricing, Cox Automotive $7B revenue

law_firm.title: Law Firm / Legal Practice
law_firm.comp_signal: LegalZoom and Rocket Lawyer are capturing the $500-2000 flat-fee market that local firms ignore, while BigLaw firms use AI intake tools to cherry-pick high-value cases leaving mid-size firms with the scraps.
law_firm.market_signal: The average law firm loses 40% of potential clients in the first 24 hours due to slow intake response. A missed consultation call at $2,500 average case value compounds into $30,000 monthly losses for a 3-attorney practice.
law_firm.tax_angle: Section 179 deduction applies to practice management software. Bar association dues and CLE costs are fully deductible. Home office deduction available for solo practitioners.
law_firm.loophole: Most law firms have zero after-hours intake. An AI intake system capturing leads between 6pm-9am owns the entire after-hours market in any metro area.
red_flags: Manual intake process losing leads to faster competitors
No online scheduling — clients call, get voicemail, call competitor
Zero CRM tracking of lead sources — marketing spend is invisible
Billing leakage — unbilled hours average 15% of total time worked

medical_clinic.title: Medical Clinic / Primary Care
medical_clinic.comp_signal: Amazon Clinic and CVS MinuteClinic are eating urgent care volume with sub-30-minute digital booking while traditional practices still use phone trees built in 2003.
medical_clinic.market_signal: The average primary care clinic loses $127,000 annually to no-shows alone. Amazon Clinic's $75 flat-fee telehealth visits are capturing the exact patient demographic that used to fill your Tuesday afternoon slots.
medical_clinic.tax_angle: HIPAA-compliant software qualifies for Section 179. Medical equipment and EHR systems fully deductible. Practice management software deductible as ordinary business expense.
medical_clinic.loophole: Most clinics have 3-week new patient wait times. A same-day or next-day AI triage and scheduling system captures every patient who gave up and went to urgent care instead.
red_flags: Phone tree intake losing patients to same-day urgent care competitors
No-show rate above 15% destroying daily revenue
Manual prior authorization costing 2-3 hours of staff time daily
Zero patient reactivation — lapsed patients never contacted
